name: Placeholder Harad Hero
text: Allies get +1 [attack] while participating in an attack with Placeholder Harad Hero. Response: After an ally and Placeholder Harad Hero participate in an attack that destroys an enemy, draw a card. Then, shuffle a copy of that ally from your discard pile into your deck. (Limit once per phase.)

name: Farmer Maggot
text: While Farmer Maggot is ready, Creature allies get +1 [willpower]. Response: When playing a Creature ally from your hand, increase its cost by 1 to give it +2 [attack] or +2 [defense] until the end of the round.

name: Abaan
text: Response: After a unique Harad ally card enters your discard pile, add that ally’s printed [willpower], [attack] and [defense] to Abaan’s corresponding attributes until the end of the phase. (Limit once per phase.)

name: Bergil
text: Response: After a hero you control defends an attack and takes no damage, ready Bergil.

name: Tamed Mûmak
text: Limit 1 per player. Forced: After Tamed Mûmak exhausts, exhaust another Harad character you control or discard Tamed Mûmak. Forced: At the end of the round, raise your threat by 1.

name: Caravan Merchant
text: Exhaust Caravan Merchant and discard an item from your hand to add 1 resource to a Harad hero’s resource pool.

name: Southron Camel
text: Play only if you control a Harad hero. Cannot have attachments. Action: Attach Southron Camel to an ally you control. (Counts as a Mount attachment with the text: “Restricted. Limit 1 per ally. Attached ally gets +1 [willpower] and does not exhaust to quest.”)

name: A Tradition Continued
text: Action: Discard a Harad ally you control to put into play a Harad ally in your discard pile.

name: Inheritance
text: Planning Action: Choose an Item attachment in your discard pile with a printed cost of 2 or less and attach it to an eligible Harad character in play.

name: Curious Fox
text: Cannot have attachments. Limit 1 per deck. Response: After Curious Fox enters play, look at the top 3 cards of the encounter deck or a player’s deck. Forced: After the active location is explored, remove Curious Fox from the game.

name: Recover Strength
text: Play into the staging area. Response: At the end of the combat phase, place 1 progress token on a ready attachment. Limit 1 progress token per attachment. Action: Remove 1 progress token from an attachment to ready it.

name: Room of Reckoning
text: Forced: After committing characters to the quest here, each player discards a card from their hand. Response: After placing progress on this quest from questing successfully, each player chooses a hero they control to gain 1 resource.

name: Wounded Guard
text: Setup: Set aside Mathom House Guard. Build the wolf-cult deck. Place all 5 Room side quests in play and draw the top X cards from the wolf-cult deck and attach them to the Room side quests as evenly as possible. X is twice the number of players. Place Search for the Lantern and Gaur the Burned in the staging area. Shuffle the 8 Mathom objectives and place 1 facedown under each of the room side quests. Shuffle the remaining Mathoms into the encounter deck.

name: Wounded Guard
text: When Revealed: Place Mathom House Guard in play under any player’s control with 2 damage on him. The players decide: We can’t let them have a head start on us! Immediately advance to stage 2A. Stop and take care of the guard. Draw a card from the Wolf-cult deck and attach it to the Room side quest with the fewest enemies attached. Heal all damage from Mathom House Guard. Skip the Quest, Travel, Encounter, and Combat phases. At the start of the Refresh phase advance to stage 2A.

name: The Search
text: When Revealed: Each player searches the encounter deck for a different location and adds it to the staging area. Shuffle the encounter deck.

name: The Search
text: Forced: After placing damage equal to or more than a Room’s quest points, add the enemies attached to it to the wolf-cult deck discard pile and remove that quest from the game. Take the Mathom under it and attach it face up to Gaur as a permanent shadow effect. After the players add a Room to the victory display, add any enemies attached to it to the staging area. The 1st player takes control of the Mathom underneath it and attaches it to a character they control, ignoring the Guarded keyword. If Gaur has 3 permanent shadow cards attached to him, the players lost the game. If the players have 3 Room quests in the victory display, the players win the game.

name: Search for the Lantern
text: Forced: At the end of the round each enemy attached to a Room places damage on that room equal to their threat. Then any enemies in the staging area place damage equal to their threat on the Room with the most damage on it. Draw a card from the wolf-cult deck and attach it to the Room with the fewest amount of damage on it.

name: Mathom House Guard
text: While Mathom House Guard has no damage on him, it gains: “Response: After placing progress on a Room after questing successfully, exhaust Mathom House Guard to place 3 progress on that Room.”

name: Gaur the Burned
text: Indestructible. Immune to player card effects and cannot leave the staging area but is considered to be engaged with each player. When Gaur the Burned attacks, he is dealt X shadow cards. X is the tens digit of the defending player’s threat. When Gaur the Burned would be defeated, remove all damage from him and reduce his [threat] to 0 until the start of the next round.

name: Bullroarer’s Armory
text: Forced: After committing characters to the quest here, the enemy attached here with the highest [attack] makes an immediate attack against the 1st player. Response: After placing progress on this quest from questing successfully, each player may play an armor or weapon attachment from their hand at no cost.

name: Vault of Tookish Deeds
text: Forced: After committing characters to the quest here, each player raises their threat by 2. Response: After placing progress on this quest from questing successfully, each player may spend 1 resource to return an event card from their discard pile to their hand.

name: Mathom Hall
text: Forced: After committing characters to the quest here, each player exhausts a character they control. Response: After placing progress on this quest from questing successfully, each player draws a card.

name: Boffin Belfry
text: Forced: After committing characters to the quest here, each player deals 2 damage among questing characters they control. Response: After placing progress on this quest from questing successfully, each player reduces their threat by 2.

name: Orc-eyes
text: Forced: If an attack by Orc-eyes would destroy a printed ally, that character gains turncoat instead. Last Gasp: Shuffle Orc-eyes into the encounter deck.

name: Local Informant
text: Last Gasp: Place 1 damage on the Room side quest with the most damage.

name: Bric-a-brac Mess
text: While Bric-a-brac Mess is the active location, only characters with 2 or more [willpower] can be committed to the quest..

name: Dusty Corner
text: While Dusty Corner is the active location, reduce each hero’s [willpower] by 1. Travel: Attach a Mathom objective from the discard pile to Dusty Corner as a guarded attachment.

name: Well-lit Hallway
text: While Well-lit Hallway is the active location, after questing successfully but before placing progress players may change which quest they are committed to without resolving the Forced effect on the new quest. Travel:  The first player engages an enemy attached to any room.

name: Pawn Shelf
text: While Pawn Shelf is the active location, each player must spend a resource before committing any characters to the quest.
shadow: Shadow: The defending character places a non-objective attachment attached to them on top of their deck.

name: Pile of Boxes
text: While Pile of Boxes is the active location, characters quest using their [attack] instead of their [willpower].

name: Tower of Books
text: While Tower of Books is the active location, each player can only commit as many characters to the quest as they have cards in hand.
shadow: Shadow: Shadow effects are immune to player card effects for the rest of this attack. Discard a card from your hand to cancel this effect.

name: Wave of Nostalgia
text: When Revealed: Resolve the Forced effect on the current quest as if you had just committed characters to the quest here. If the current quest does not have the Room Trait, this card gains surge.
shadow: Shadow: Either choose and exhaust a different character you control to declare them as the defender for this attack, or raise your threat by 4.

name: Speed up You Maggots
text: When Revealed: Place damage on the current quest equal to the threat of the enemies attached to it and add their threat to the staging area. If no damage is placed this way this card gains surge. The players may make a new Room the current quest and resolve its Forced effect as if they just committed characters to the quest.
shadow: Shadow: Excess damage dealt by this attack is placed on the Room side quest with the most damage on it.

name: Get to Work
text: When Revealed: Draw a card from the Wolf-cult deck and attach it to the Room side quest with the least amount of damage on it. If you cannot, place damage on the room with the most attached enemies equal to their threat.
shadow: Shadow: Attacking enemy gets +1 [attack] (+2 [attack] instead if the players explored a side quest this round).

name: Useless Minions
text: When Revealed: Deal 1 damage to each Wolf-cult minion engaged with a player.
shadow: Shadow: Deal 1 damage to a Wolf-cult enemy engaged with you. If this destroys a Wolf-cult enemy, trigger their Last Gasp effect twice.

name: White Wolf Pelt
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Action: Exhaust White Wolf Pelt to place 1 progress token on a Room or location in play.
shadow: Shadow: Remove a progress token from a quest or location in play.

name: Fishing Tackle
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Exhaust Fishing Tackle to draw a card.
shadow: Shadow: Deal 1 damage to a character you control.

name: Song Book
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Attached character gains the Minstrel Trait and does not exhaust to quest.
shadow: Shadow: Exhaust a character you control.

name: Bilbo’s Mithril Shirt
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Attached character gets +1 [defense] and +1 hit point.
shadow: Shadow: Discard the top 2 cards from your deck.

name: Bullroarer’s Wooden Club
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Restricted. Attached character gets +1 [attack]. After attached character destroys an enemy place 1 progress on a location in the staging area.

name: Doilies
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Exhaust Doilies to discard 2 resources on a hero you control. That hero gains 3 resources.
shadow: Shadow: Discard a resource from a hero you control.

name: Tobold Hornblower’s Pipe Collection
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Exhaust Tobold Hornblower’s Pipe Collection to reduce your threat by 1.

name: Bucca’s Shovel
text: Attach to a character controlled by the 1st player. Restricted. Response: After attached character destroys a Wolf-cult enemy, exhaust Bucca’s Shovel to ignore the effects of that enemy’s Last Gasp effect.
shadow: Shadow: The defending character gets –1 [defense] until the end of the phase.

name: The Mathom House Room Rules
text: Rules and FAQ regarding Room side quests for playtesting. Enemies attached to a Room side quest do not contribute their threat to the staging area and are not considered to be in the staging area. Enemies attached to Rooms that are not the current quest are immune to player card effects. They do not make engagement checks but enemies in the room chosen as the main quest that round can be optionally engaged during the encounter phase. When a Room leaves play, flip over the Mathom underneath it. If the Room was removed from the game due to damage, attach the mathom to Gaur as a permanent shadow effect and discard any enemies attached. If the room was added to the victory display due to being explored with progress tokens, attach the mathom to a character the first player controls and add attached enemies to the staging area. The wolf-cult deck has its own discard pile and any wolf-cult enemies that would go to a discard pile are placed there. If the wolf-cult deck is ever empty, shuffle its discard pile back into the deck.

name: Fanged Thief
text: Last Gasp: Shuffle a Mathom objective you control back into the encounter deck.

name: Friendly Competition
text: Play only if you control Legolas and Gimli. Response: After you destroy an enemy (choose 1): Place 2 resource tokens on a copy of Keeping Count, or search your collection for 2 copies of Keeping Count and attach one of them to Gimli and the other one to Legolas.
